Gestión de la Partida Asignada: Responsable de la organización, limpieza y funcionamiento eficiente de la partida o sección de cocina asignada (ej. plancha, pescados, carnes, guarniciones).
Servicio de Buffet: Participar activamente en la preparación, reposición y presentación de los alimentos en las áreas de buffet, asegurando su frescura, atractivo visual y disponibilidad constante durante los servicios.
Showcooking: Colaborar en las estaciones de "showcooking", demostrando habilidades culinarias y ofreciendo una experiencia interactiva y de calidad a los huéspedes.
Control de Calidad: Vigilar la calidad de los ingredientes y productos terminados, asegurando que cumplen con los criterios de sabor, textura, temperatura y presentación.
Higiene y Seguridad Alimentaria: Mantener un estricto cumplimiento de las normativas de higiene y seguridad alimentaria (APPCC), incluyendo la limpieza y desinfección del área de trabajo, utensilios y equipos.
Gestión de Stock y Pedidos: Colaborar en el control de inventario de materias primas de su partida, reportando necesidades de reposición y minimizando el desperdicio.
Colaboración en Equipo: Trabajar de manera coordinada con el resto del equipo de cocina (jefes de partida, ayudantes, otros cocineros) y con el personal de sala para garantizar un servicio fluido y eficiente.
Innovación y Creatividad: Aportar ideas y sugerencias para la mejora de menús, técnicas de preparación y presentación de platos, adaptándose a las tendencias culinarias.
Adaptabilidad: Ser capaz de adaptarse a diferentes tipos de cocina y servicios, según las necesidades del hotel y la temporada.


Si eres un profesional apasionado por la cocina, buscas un entorno de trabajo estimulante y deseas crecer en una empresa líder en el sector hotelero, ¡te invitamos a formar parte de ALEGRIA Group!

DESCRIPCIóN

ALEGRIA Group es una compañía hotelera consolidada y en constante expansión, reconocida por ofrecer experiencias excepcionales a nuestros huéspedes y por promover un ambiente de trabajo positivo y profesional. Con una sólida trayectoria en el sector, nos distinguimos por la calidad de nuestros servicios, la innovación en nuestras propuestas y el compromiso de nuestro equipo humano. Buscamos constantemente talento que comparta nuestra pasión por la hospitalidad y que desee crecer junto a nosotros en un entorno dinámico y gratificante.


Descripción de la Posición:

En ALEGRIA Group, la limpieza y el confort de nuestras habitaciones y apartamentos son pilares fundamentales de nuestra oferta hotelera. Como Camarera/o de Pisos, serás una pieza clave en la garantía de la máxima satisfacción de nuestros huéspedes, asegurando que cada espacio cumpla con los más altos estándares de higiene, orden y presentación. Tu misión será mantener impecables las habitaciones, zonas comunes y apartamentos asignados, contribuyendo directamente a la excelente reputación de nuestros establecimientos.


Funciones Principales:

Limpieza y Desinfección de Habitaciones y Apartamentos: Realizar la limpieza diaria y exhaustiva de las habitaciones y apartamentos asignados, incluyendo el cambio de lencería (sábanas, toallas), vaciado de papeleras, limpieza de baños (sanitarios, espejos, suelos), y aspirado/barrido de suelos.
Mantenimiento del Orden y la Presentación: Asegurar que las habitaciones y apartamentos queden perfectamente ordenados, que los amenities estén completos y correctamente dispuestos, y que la presentación general invite al descanso y la comodidad del huésped.
Limpieza de Zonas Comunes: Colaborar en la limpieza y mantenimiento de las zonas comunes del hotel, como pasillos, ascensores, escaleras y vestíbulos, garantizando un ambiente agradable para todos.
Gestión de Lencería y Amenities: Controlar el stock de lencería y amenities en carros de limpieza y en las habitaciones, informando de las necesidades de reposición.
Identificación y Reporte de Incidencias: Detectar cualquier desperfecto, avería o anomalía en las habitaciones o zonas comunes (mobiliario, instalaciones, etc.) y reportarlo de manera inmediata al departamento correspondiente.
Atención a las Necesidades del Huésped: Atender de forma amable y eficiente las peticiones o necesidades que los huéspedes puedan tener relacionadas con la limpieza y el mantenimiento de su habitación.
Cumplimiento de Normativas: Adherirse a las normativas internas de ALEGRIA Group en materia de seguridad, higiene y salud laboral.
Colaboración con el Equipo: Mantener una comunicación fluida y colaborativa con el equipo de pisos y otros departamentos del hotel para optimizar el servicio.


REQUISITOS

Experiencia Profesional: Se requiere una experiencia mínima de dos (2) años en puestos similares de limpieza y mantenimiento en el sector hotelero, preferiblemente en hoteles o apartamentos turísticos.
Conocimientos de Limpieza: Dominio de técnicas de limpieza y desinfección aplicadas a entornos hoteleros, conocimiento de productos de limpieza y su uso seguro.
Atención al Detalle: Capacidad demostrada para mantener altos estándares de limpieza y organización, prestando especial atención al detalle.
Organización y Gestión del Tiempo: Habilidad para organizar y gestionar eficientemente las tareas asignadas, optimizando los tiempos de limpieza para cumplir con los estándares del hotel.
Condición Física: Buena condición física para realizar las tareas propias del puesto, que implican movimiento constante, carga de peso (lencería) y posturas.
Proactividad y Autonomía: Capacidad para trabajar de forma autónoma, identificando tareas y resolviendo problemas de manera proactiva.
Habilidades de Comunicación: Habilidad para comunicarse de forma clara y respetuosa con compañeros y, puntualmente, con huéspedes.
Actitud Positiva: Disposición para el trabajo en equipo, actitud amable y vocación de servicio.
Flexibilidad Horaria: Disponibilidad para trabajar en diferentes turnos y fines de semana según las necesidades del servicio.

Se Valorará:

Formación en Hostelería o Turismo.
Conocimiento de otros idiomas.
Experiencia en el manejo de maquinaria de limpieza profesional.

Company Description


SO/ Sotogrande es un homenaje a la moda y el diseño, la gastronomía, el bienestar y el golf en la costa andaluza. Rodeado de verdes colinas y valles con vistas al Mediterráneo, SO/ Sotogrande Spa & Golf Resort se encuentra en un entorno único. El complejo ha recuperado el espíritu acogedor y la esencia del antiguo Cortijo de Santa María de la Higuera, transformándolo en un lugar idílico donde familias y amigos celebran la vida, disfrutan juntos de la serenidad y adoptan un estilo de vida saludable y contemporáneo.

Es el destino preferido en la Costa del Sol para los amantes del golf, ya que goza de una ubicación privilegiada rodeada de los siete mejores campos de golf de Europa. Cuenta con 152 habitaciones y 36 suites e instalaciones excepcionales, entre las que se incluyen: 3 piscinas al aire libre, jardines privados, un club infantil, un spa y gimnasio de 3500 m2 y luminosas salas para eventos con vistas. Su oferta gastronómica consta de 5 restaurantes y bares, donde se puede disfrutar de los sabores tradicionales de Andalucía, presentados con un toque fresco, y cócteles de autor.

DESCRIPCIóN

Objetivo del puesto

Atender las dificultades de adaptación de las personas solicitantes de protección internacional, refugiadas y apátridas e impulsar el desarrollo de competencias y habilidades psicosociales, mediante el desarrollo de procesos individuales y/o colectivos de evaluación, diagnóstico y seguimiento psicológico, en estrecha colaboración y coordinación con los servicios psicológicos de referencia, con el fin de garantizar un estado óptimo de la salud y la preparación para la vida autónoma de los/las beneficiarios/as del Programa. En dependencia directa del/ de la Responsable Territorial del Área o Coordinación Territorial / Estatal. ﻿

Funciones principales

Identificar las necesidades de atención psicológica/psiquiátrica del colectivo de atención.
Detectar, evaluar y realizar el seguimiento de posibles vulnerabilidades o necesidades particulares de las personas atendidas desde el Servicio.
Derivar a recursos de atención psicológica/psiquiátrica para situaciones específicas que requieran de una atención especializada en la materia concreta (violencia de género, trastornos de salud mental grave, consumo, trastornos alimenticios, etc.).
Realizar atenciones grupales donde se trabajen, desde una perspectiva integral, las expectativas, promoción de la salud, autoestima, gestión del estrés, estrategias de afrontamiento y resiliencia, trauma, culpa, duelo y atenciones psicológicas clínicas, cuando así se valore.
Elaborar informes psicológicos que resulten de utilidad para acreditar la situación de la persona atendida, a fin de ser incluida en el pertinente itinerario y, en su caso, que puedan ser empleados para acreditar ante las diferentes administraciones la vulnerabilidad psicológica de la persona atendida.
Realizar acompañamientos a recursos especializados, en los casos que así se valore.
Llevar a cabo actuaciones encaminadas a la prevención de eventuales daños psicológicos mayores.
Realizar diagnósticos psicológicos (según criterios del DSM-V), con especial conocimiento del trastorno de estrés postraumático.
Realizar mediación en situaciones de conflictos e intervención de urgencia en situaciones de crisis.
Acercar a los/las profesionales de la Organización, de una manera sencilla, a la realidad psicológica de la migración y el asilo (nuevas publicaciones y estudios).
Elaborar informes, memorias, adaptaciones, propuestas de mejora de planes y proyectos relacionados con el programa de atención y gestión administrativa derivada.
Realizar registro en la base de datos SIRIA, base de datos de la Organización CEARNET y cualquier otra dependiente de la gestión del Programa.

DESCRIPCIóN

¡Únete a Eosol Group como Piloto de Drones!


Eosol Group, empresa líder en soluciones innovadoras, está en búsqueda de un Piloto de Drones altamente cualificado y experimentado para unirse a nuestro equipo. Si posees una sólida formación técnica, una pasión por la tecnología UAV y cumples con los requisitos de certificación y experiencia, te invitamos a formar parte de un entorno profesional dinámico y desafiante.


Sobre Eosol Group:


En Eosol Group, nos dedicamos a impulsar la transformación digital y la eficiencia operativa a través de la tecnología. Nuestra misión es ofrecer soluciones de vanguardia que aporten valor real a nuestros clientes en diversos sectores.


Responsabilidades del Puesto:


Como Piloto de Drones en Eosol Group, serás responsable de:


Operación de UAVs: Ejecutar misiones programadas de vuelo, incluyendo inspección de infraestructuras, vigilancia de áreas, y apoyo técnico en proyectos diversos.
Mantenimiento y Preparación: Llevar a cabo la preparación, chequeo exhaustivo y mantenimiento básico de las aeronaves no tripuladas (UAVs) y sus cargas de pago (sensores, cámaras, etc.).
Planificación de Vuelos: Diseñar y planificar misiones de vuelo de acuerdo con la normativa vigente de AESA (Agencia Estatal de Seguridad Aérea) y EASA (Agencia de la Unión Europea para la Seguridad Aérea), garantizando la máxima seguridad y eficiencia.
Gestión Documental: Administrar y mantener al día toda la documentación operativa relacionada con los vuelos, incluyendo partes de vuelo, registros de mantenimiento y licencias.
Coordinación de Equipos: Colaborar estrechamente con el equipo de proyecto, otros departamentos y los clientes para asegurar la correcta ejecución de las misiones y la satisfacción del cliente.
Soporte Técnico: Brindar apoyo en las fases de pruebas, validaciones y despliegues de nuevas tecnologías y sistemas en campo.
Integración de Sistemas: Participar activamente en el soporte para la integración de sensores avanzados, sistemas de comunicación y estaciones de control terrestres.


Requisitos Técnicos Mínimos:


Buscamos candidatos con el siguiente perfil:


Certificación de Piloto de Drones: Poseer certificación de piloto de drones conforme a la normativa EASA. Se requiere categoría A2 o STS (con habilitación específica para STS-01 / STS-02).
Experiencia Profesional: Contar con un mínimo de 4 años de experiencia demostrable operando drones en un entorno profesional.
Conocimientos Técnicos: Sólidos conocimientos en:
Planificación de misiones y trayectorias de vuelo.
Procedimientos de seguridad, protocolos de emergencia y gestión de riesgos.
Mantenimiento básico y troubleshooting de sistemas UAV.
Carnet de Conducir: Disponer de carnet de conducir tipo B.
Disponibilidad: Flexibilidad y disponibilidad para realizar desplazamientos geográficos según las necesidades del proyecto.





Formación Académica y Certificaciones Adicionales:




Se valorará positivamente Formación Profesional (FP) o Grado Universitario en áreas como Electrónica, Aeronáutica, Telecomunicaciones, Robótica o campos afines.
Certificaciones adicionales: Radiofonista Aeronáutico, Formación en operaciones BVLOS (valorable) y PRL (trabajos en campo / entornos industriales).





Habilidades Clave:




Capacidad de análisis y resolución de problemas.
Excelentes habilidades de comunicación y trabajo en equipo.
Alto sentido de la responsabilidad y atención al detalle.
Adaptabilidad y proactividad.


¿Por qué unirte a Eosol Group?


Ofrecemos un ambiente de trabajo estimulante, oportunidades de desarrollo profesional, participación en proyectos innovadores y la posibilidad de formar parte de una empresa en constante crecimiento.


Palabras Clave: Piloto drones, UAVs, inspección, vigilancia, normativa AESA.

Descripción de la empresa


Como referente de la transformación ecológica, en Veolia ofrecemos soluciones para la gestión eficiente de las actividades de agua, residuos y energía, impulsando el desarrollo sostenible de las ciudades, industrias y agricultura.

Descripción del empleo

Será la persona encargada de dar soporte a los especialistas y responsables de operaciones en redes de agua potable y/o saneamiento, asegurando la correcta aplicación de las directrices y la operativa de las explotaciones de su ámbito territorial, de acuerdo con los principios éticos y las políticas corporativas de la compañía.

Funciones principales:

Dar soporte en la ejecución y control del mantenimiento preventivo y correctivo de las instalaciones.

Colaborar en inspecciones e informes relacionados con el control de calidad del agua.

Colaborar en la elaboración de informes destinados a las administraciones públicas.
Colaborar en el cumplimiento de las actividades relacionadas con los Sistemas de Gestión implantados en los servicios de abastecimiento y/o saneamiento.
Dar soporte en la ejecución y control de las obras de reparación, ampliación y/o mejora de las infraestructuras de los servicios de abastecimiento y/o saneamiento.

Company Description


SO/ Sotogrande is a tribute to fashion & design, gastronomy, wellness and golf on the Andalusian coast. Surrounded by green hills and valleys overlooking the Mediterranean, SO/ Sotogrande Spa & Golf Resort is located in a unique enclave. The resort has recovered the welcoming spirit and essence of the old Cortijo de Santa María de la Higuera, turning it into an idyllic place where families and friends celebrate life, enjoy serenity and a healthy and contemporary lifestyle together.

It is the preferred destination on the Costa del Sol for golf lovers, enjoying a privileged location surrounded by the 7 best golf courses in Europe. It has 152 rooms and 36 suites and exceptional facilities among which you can enjoy: 3 outdoor swimming pools, private gardens, Kid's Club, 3,500 m2 of spa and fitness and luminous event rooms with views. Its gastronomic offer consists of 5 Restaurants & Bars, where you can enjoy the traditional flavors of Andalusia, proposals with a renewed vision and signature cocktails.

Meeting business stakeholders to understand the problems they wish to address

Titulo: Marine Structural Engineer –Mid to Senior Level
Descripcion_Completa:
Moffatt & Nichol specializes in large complex waterfront infrastructure projects and is recognized as one of the worldwide leaders in this field. We are seeking a Marine Structural Engineer to design waterfront and marine infrastructure projects, including marinas, vessel mooring and berthing structures, bulkheads, breakwaters, and shore protection structures. The ideal candidate will have 6–15 years of experience delivering projects from conceptual design through detailed engineering

About Moffatt & Nichol:

Moffatt & Nichol is a leading global infrastructure advisor specializing in the planning and design of facilities that shape and serve our coastlines, harbours, and rivers. Reflecting Moffatt & Nichol’s commitment to design innovation since 1945, Engineering News-Record (ENR) has ranked the company No. 1 in Marine and Port Facilities and among the Top 50 Designers in International Markets.

Moffatt & Nichol's professional staff includes engineers, planners, scientists, and architects who serve our global client base from offices in Europe, the Americas, and the Pacific Rim. The firm provides clients worldwide with customized service and a level of excellence that have become the firm’s hallmark in three primary practice areas—coastal, environmental, and water resources; ports and harbours; and transportation, bridges, and rail.

Duties and responsibilities:

Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions.

Structural analysis, design, and detailing for concrete, steel, timber, and composite materials.

Inspection and rehabilitation of waterfront structures.

Delivery of urban waterfront and coastal infrastructure projects.

Designing quay walls, jetties, trestles, near-shore platforms, mooring/breasting structures, breakwaters and floodwalls.

Knowledge of liquid, gas, container and bulk terminals and shoreline protection systems.

Management of marine structural projects and multidisciplinary teams.

Provide construction support and resolve technical queries.

Knowledge of shoreline protection systems and breakwater design.

Awareness of project budgets, procurement strategies, scheduling, and preparation of technical documentation.

Health & Safety Responsibilities:


All employees are expected to support a safe working environment by following health and safety laws, company policies, and procedures. This includes identifying and reporting risks, promoting safe practices, and contributing to continuous improvement in workplace safety.

Other duties:

Please note this job posting is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

Qualifications:

Degree: BEng/MEng in Civil or Structural Engineering (Master’s preferred).

Chartership:

Chartered Engineer (CEng) with ICE or IStructE, or working towards chartership.

Experience: 6–15 years in marine structural engineering design and project management within a consultancy or marine-related client environment.

Familiarity with UK design codes (Eurocodes, BS standards) and international guidelines (PIANC, BS6349).

Excellent communication skills and ability to work collaboratively.

Experience with Network Rail, port authorities, or defense projects is advantageous.

All new hires will be required to successfully complete and pass a pre-employment (post offer) background check in compliance with NIST 800-171

Working conditions:

Sedentary for prolonged period of time, interact with clients, and travel occasionally.

Primarily office-based (Farnham, London or Exeter), with occasional site visits to ports, harbours, and coastal locations.

Site visits may involve navigating uneven terrain and working near water.

PURPOSE OF THE JOB
The role is responsible for the upkeep, maintenance and performance of terminal equipment and facilities in order to maintain smooth operational conditions.
The incumbent shall ensure inspections and maintenance regime for plant equipment and installations is implemented as per the annual maintenance plan and strategy set by Engineering.
The Maintenance Engineer will be responsible specifically in the areas of:
Maintenance
Equipment compliance
Cost Control
CAPEX
Budgeting & Cost Control
Monitoring Regulatory Compliance
CONTROL PERFORMANCE INDICATORS (Tools methods used by management to determine sustained effectiveness of control)
Terminal equipment availability and reliability
Accurate cost forecasting / budgeting for projects
Frequency and severity of incidents due to maintenance non-compliance
Planned Budget vs. Actual costs
Number of outstanding CAPEX projects not concluded within time
Any other items agreed to in the performance contract.
MAIN DUTIES
Single point of accountability (SPA) for Operations maintenance regime - develop a Maintenance Plan for all Terminals, Consignment and Mine Sites that covers inspections, calibration, testing and servicing of equipment and installations.
Submits periodic maintenance and inspection reports as required and monitors record keeping with regard to maintenance regime and operations excellence requirements.
Works with Terminal/Site Mangers to ensure that servicing of all equipment is carried out and that records of performance and history are maintained as per Operations Manual requirements.
Supervise maintenance of terminal and storage equipment for bulk fuel tank farm, pipelines, pump stations and all associated equipment and facilities on site.
Responsible for implementation, training and management of the Annual Maintenance budget and TAG maintenance system and providing information as required by Management
Planning all maintenance (via the TAG system) activity from equipment purchasing through to day to day activities through to feasibility studies to support process improvements, the continued operations and expansion of the terminal.
Participates in Risk Assessment of Terminal/Sites to develop the Major Accident/Incident Prevention (MAIP) document.
Supervise CAPEX projects effecting modifications from scoping, design stage through to procurement, commissioning and handover operations regarding any process improvements and expansion plans associated with the terminal and sites
Ensures that all requirements of SAPS, Environmental Management System for ISO 14001 and the Quality Management System for ISO 9001 are implemented in order to achieve and sustain full regulatory and company compliance and certification.
Ensure maintenance cost control by monitoring and analysing budget against expenditure and advise deviation from planned maintenance budget.
Perform any other duties as assigned by the Terminal Manager.
KEY CHALENGES FACED ON THE ROLE
Optimizing terminal equipment availability and reliability and ensuring that maintenance costs are within budget.
Ensuring that CAPEX projects are executed timely
Ensuring competence in managing specialized equipment
RELATIONSHIPS

Operations Team
Engineering Team
Procurement
Finance Team
EHS Manager
Internal Auditors
External
External Auditors

Contractors (Engineering & Transport)

EDUCATION AND TRAINING
Education background
Degree in Engineering with minimum 5 years experience, 2 of which should be in terminal or related operations
At least 5 years’ experience as a technician in the fuel terminal with supervisory experience or degree in Engineering with 2 years’ experience in terminal or related operations.
At least 5 years’ experience as a terminal/fuel equipment technician and supervisory experience or degree in Engineering with 2 years’ experience in terminal operations.
Computer literate
Oil & Gas Industry an added advantage
Competencies and experience
Experience gained within fuel storage or terminal maintenance operations.
Ability to supervise a team of Technicians, contractors and other support staff.
Extensive knowledge of depot procedures and operations.
Experience in supervising HSEQ compliance
Ability to work with diverse teams and third-party negotiations.
Petroleum product knowledge will be an added advantage
Computer literate
Other
English language required

Based at the oil terminal site with potential exposure to hazardous materials and confined spaces.
May require shift work, on-call availability, and emergency response participation.

Job Description

A senior role for an experienced Platform Engineer, join our team to work on exciting infrastructure projects and help advance our service offering.

Key Responsibilities

Team Leadership & Development

Support and guide platform engineering team members

Mentor junior engineers and facilitate knowledge sharing

Prioritise team tasks and coordinate project activities

Technical Ownership

Design, build, and maintain cloud architectures and SaaS products

Continuously improve operational metrics and system reliability
Define and monitor Service Level Objectives (SLO) and Service Level Indicators (SLI)

Lead incident response and post-mortem analysis

Shape product definitions and service offerings for internal and external stakeholders

Required knowledge, skills, and experience

Technical Skills

Security Operations (SecOps) and security best practices

Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) principles and practices
Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
Cloud-native architecture and container orchestration (Kubernetes, Docker)
CI/CD pipelines and automation frameworks
Drupal CMS platform management and optimization
CDN/WAF configuration and management (VCL, edge computing)
Observability and monitoring (metrics, logging, tracing)
Network architecture and troubleshooting

Linux system administration and containerization technologies

Soft Skills

Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities

Team leadership and mentoring experience
Ownership mindset and proactive approach

Excellent communication skills in cross-functional teams

English proficiency at B2 level or higher

Experience

3+ years as a Platform Engineer

OR 5+ years in DevOps or Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)

What we offer

A full-time position with a competitive salary.

Employees are encouraged to attend industry events and conferences for networking, learning, and personal growth.
Quality time for yourself is a must! We care about work/life balance. That’s why we stick to 40 hours a week and offer flexible working hours and home office schemes.
Work with brilliant colleagues in flat hierarchies on exciting and challenging projects.
Smart offices and a creative working environment.
An environment in which independent work and the contribution of new ideas is welcomed and valued.
Great training and personal development opportunities with a comprehensive career track.

A yearly company retreat and team building events in our office locations throughout the year.

An apartment in sunny Conil (Spain) that you can use during the year.

Work location

This is a remote position for an engineer based in Spain. However, our teams work closely together and we encourage employees to visit our offices in Frankfurt, Berlin or Conil whenever possible.

About us

We're a digital agency built on technical excellence. For over a decade, 1xINTERNET has been building sophisticated web platforms and digital experiences for clients across Europe, with a reputation for tackling the complex challenges others shy away from. We specialise in enterprise Drupal solutions, native apps, hosting services and cloud infrastructure – the kind of work where engineering excellence isn't optional, it's the baseline. Headquartered in Germany with a distributed team across Europe of 85+ people, we combine German engineering rigor with the flexibility and autonomy of a modern tech company.

¡Haz crecer tu pasión por la veterinaria con Clinicanimal!

En Clinicanimal, somos más que una red de clínicas veterinarias. Somos un equipo que comparte una #pasiónauténtica por el cuidado animal y que trabaja cada día con el compromiso de ofrecer una atención de calidad a nuestros pacientes y sus familias.

¿Te gustaría formar parte de un proyecto en constante crecimiento, donde tu desarrollo profesional y personal son nuestra prioridad?

Únete a un entorno que fomenta la formación continua, el trabajo en equipo y el bienestar de sus integrantes.

Crecimiento continuo: Acceso a planes formativos personalizados y oportunidades de desarrollo interno.

Un día adicional de vacaciones tras 5 años en la compañía, porque valoramos tu compromiso.

Tu día especial: Posibilidad de descansar el día de tu cumpleaños.

Movilidad interna: Si buscas nuevos horizontes, facilitamos el traslado entre nuestras clínicas en diferentes localidades.

Apoyo emocional: Programa de ayuda al empleado/a para brindarte apoyo psicológico en temas personales y profesionales.

Pertenencia a un gran equipo: Sé parte de un grupo líder en servicios veterinarios, con presencia en más de 70 centros en España y Portugal.

¿Qué buscamos en ti?







Formación académica en Veterinaria.
Al menos 4 años de experiencia en consultas de pequeños animales
Experiencia en cirugías básicas, castraciones y nodulectomias.
Manejo de herramientas informáticas a nivel de usuario

Te unirás a nuestro centro en Puerto Real, como especialista en veterinaria a jornada completa y temporal, encargándote de prestar servicios veterinarios integrales, desde el diagnóstico hasta la cirugía y la medicina preventiva.

Main Purpose:
El Planificador de Mantenimiento es responsable principalmente de:

Desarrollar y ejecutar planes de mantenimiento que aborden las necesidades inmediatas de mantenimiento de equipos, instalaciones y sistemas.
Asegurar un tiempo de inactividad mínimo y una resolución rápida de los problemas de mantenimiento para mantener nuestras operaciones funcionando de manera confiable y con un rendimiento óptimo.
Knowledge Skills and Abilities, Key Responsibilities:
Salud y Seguridad en el Trabajo

Como empleado de Puma Energy, mientras se encuentre en el trabajo, usted debe:
Velar razonablemente por su propia salud y seguridad, así como por la de las demás personas, incluyendo aquellas bajo su supervisión o dirección, que puedan verse afectadas por sus acciones u omisiones en el lugar de trabajo.
Acatar toda instrucción razonable emitida por su supervisor en relación con asuntos de salud, seguridad y medio ambiente, conforme a la legislación aplicable.
Cumplir en todo momento con la Política de HSSE de Puma Energy, la Gestión de Riesgos, las Políticas de Drogas y Alcohol, así como con los Estándares y Procedimientos de HSSE establecidos por la compañía.
Reconocer que, al desempeñar funciones que impliquen instruir, dirigir, asistir, asesorar o brindar servicios que afecten la salud o el bienestar de otros, asume la condición de persona responsable, debiendo cumplir plenamente con las obligaciones establecidas por la legislación de Salud y Seguridad en el Trabajo.
Como miembro del equipo de liderazgo, usted es responsable de garantizar el cumplimiento y la promoción de la cultura HSSE dentro de su área de responsabilidad, asegurando que:
Todas las prácticas, procesos y objetivos de HSSE sean claramente establecidos, comprendidos y cumplidos por sus empleados, contratistas y visitantes.
La Política de HSSE de Puma Energy, junto con las políticas de Gestión de Riesgos, Drogas y Alcohol, y los Estándares y Procedimientos aplicables, sean comunicados de forma oportuna y efectiva a todo el personal, contratistas y visitantes bajo su supervisión.
Todos los empleados, contratistas y visitantes cumplan plenamente con los requisitos de las Políticas, Estándares y Procedimientos de HSSE de Puma Energy durante el desarrollo de sus actividades laborales.
Se identifiquen, evalúen y controlen los peligros presentes en el lugar de trabajo que puedan afectar la salud, seguridad o bienestar de empleados, contratistas y visitantes.
Las Evaluaciones de Riesgo aplicables a las tareas ejecutadas por empleados y contratistas sean elaboradas, revisadas y mantenidas conforme a los requisitos y estándares de HSSE de Puma Energy.
Cualquier preocupación o situación relacionada con HSSE reportada por empleados, contratistas o visitantes sea gestionada y resuelta de manera oportuna, garantizando una respuesta adecuada y el seguimiento correspondiente.
La planificación y ejecución de proyectos incluyan una evaluación documentada de los riesgos HSSE asociados, asegurando la aplicación de la jerarquía de controles para reducir dichos riesgos al nivel más bajo razonablemente posible (ALARP)
Cumplimiento del Sistema de Gestión de Calidad
Cumplir y apoyar activamente la implementación del Sistema de Gestión de Calidad (SGC) en todos los aspectos de su función.
Adoptar e incorporar los principios de mejora continua en todas las actividades y procesos bajo su responsabilidad, promoviendo la eficiencia y la innovación.
Asegurar que se mantenga y promueva la conciencia sobre los requisitos y expectativas de los clientes, tanto internos como externos, en toda la organización.
Revisar, monitorear y proporcionar retroalimentación constructiva sobre la efectividad del Sistema de Gestión de Calidad, contribuyendo a su fortalecimiento.
Participar activamente en los procesos de comunicación que respalden el SGC, fomentando la colaboración y la generación de resultados positivos para la empresa, los clientes y las partes interesadas clave.
Responsabilidades clave:
Priorización de mantenimiento: colaborar con los equipos de mantenimiento y las partes interesadas para priorizar las tareas a corto y largo plazo, considerando su urgencia, criticidad e impacto potencial en las operaciones.
Programación de mantenimiento: desarrollar planes y cronogramas detallados, asegurando que las actividades de mantenimiento se ejecuten de manera oportuna, eficiente y alineada con las necesidades operativas
Coordinación de recursos: trabajar estrechamente con el personal de mantenimiento, contratistas y demás partes interesadas para asignar los recursos necesarios —incluyendo mano de obra calificada, repuestos y equipos— que garanticen la ejecución efectiva de las tareas programadas
Respuesta a contingencias: atender con rapidez las solicitudes urgentes de mantenimiento y brindar apoyo ante fallas imprevistas, con el fin de minimizar los tiempos de inactividad y mantener la continuidad operativa.
Mantenimiento preventivo: participar en la planificación, programación y seguimiento del mantenimiento preventivo, orientado a reducir el riesgo de fallas inesperadas y prolongar la vida útil de los equipos e instalaciones.
Gestión documental: mantener registros precisos y actualizados de todas las actividades de mantenimiento, incluyendo órdenes de trabajo, tiempos de ejecución, materiales utilizados y documentación de soporte requerida según los estándares de mantenimiento preventivo
Comunicación y seguimiento: mantener informadas a las partes interesadas sobre el progreso de las actividades, posibles retrasos y ajustes requeridos en los planes de mantenimiento, promoviendo la transparencia y la coordinación efectiva.
Cumplimiento normativo: cumplimiento normativo: Asegurar que todas las actividades de mantenimiento cumplan con las normativas de la industria, los estándares de seguridad, los requisitos ambientales y las políticas corporativas de Puma Energy.
Otras responsabilidades: ejecutar cualquier otra tarea asignada por su gerente, relacionada con las funciones de mantenimiento o con los objetivos operativos del área.
Requisitos:
Educación:
Diploma de secundaria o equivalente requerido.
Certificado de oficio o formación técnica requerida.
Certificación en planificación de mantenimiento preferida.
Experiencia, habilidades y competencias:
Bilingüe, dominio del idioma local e inglés.
Experiencia previa en planificación y coordinación de mantenimiento.
Sólido conocimiento de prácticas de mantenimiento y confiabilidad de equipos.
Capacidad para pensar rápidamente y desarrollar soluciones para necesidades urgentes de mantenimiento.
Habilidades organizativas para priorizar tareas, gestionar el tiempo eficazmente y cumplir plazos ajustados.
Flexibilidad para manejar situaciones inesperadas y adaptar planes según los cambios en los requisitos de mantenimiento.
Atención meticulosa a los detalles para asegurar documentación precisa y ejecución exacta de planes de mantenimiento.
Competencia en el uso de sistemas computarizados de gestión de mantenimiento (CMMS) y software relevante para planificación e informes de mantenimiento.
Excelentes habilidades de comunicación para interactuar eficazmente con equipos diversos, proveedores y partes interesadas.

Titulo: Civil Engineer - Mid to Senior Level
Descripcion_Completa:
Moffatt & Nichol specializes in large complex waterfront infrastructure projects and is recognized as one of the worldwide leaders in this field. We are actively looking for a Civil Engineer to join either our UK or Spain office. As part of the Civil Group, the Civil Engineer will specialize in the detailed design of marine infrastructure, with a focus on ports, terminals, and waterfront developments. This role involves technical design of grading, drainage, and wet utilities, contributing to multidisciplinary teams and delivering high-quality engineering solutions for international and domestic clients.

About Moffatt & Nichol:

Moffatt & Nichol is a leading infrastructure advisor specialising in the planning and design of facilities that shape and serve our coastlines. Reflecting Moffatt & Nichol’s commitment to design innovation since 1945, Engineering News-Record (ENR) has ranked the company No. 1 design firm for Marine and Port Facilities in the United States and No. 7 internationally.

Moffatt & Nichol's professional staff includes engineers, planners, scientists, and architects who serve our global client base from offices in Europe, the Americas, the Middle East and Asia Pacific. The firm provides clients worldwide with customised service and a level of excellence that have become the firm’s hallmark in three primary practise areas—coastal, environmental, and water resources; ports and harbours; and transportation, bridges, and rail.

Duties and responsibilities:

Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions.

Lead or support detailed design of marine onshore infrastructure including access roads, grading plans, stormwater systems, and utility networks
Prepare and review engineering drawings, specifications, and technical reports using industry-standard design software
Develop design packages for planning approvals, tender documentation, and construction
Coordinate with internal disciplines (structural, geotechnical, coastal, electrical) and external consultants to ensure integrated and constructible design solutions
Interface with clients and stakeholders to understand project requirements and provide technical input during design phases
Conduct feasibility studies and technical assessments including topographic, hydrologic, and geotechnical analysis
Ensure compliance with relevant codes, standards, and regulatory frameworks, including port authority and environmental requirements
Support QA/QC processes and contribute to internal design reviews
Assist in preparing cost estimates and design schedules
Mentor junior engineers and contribute to technical development across the team

Software Tools & Technical Proficiency

AutoCAD Civil 3D – for grading, alignment, and utility design
MicroDrainage / InfoDrainage – for stormwater and drainage modeling
HEC-RAS / HEC-HMS – for hydrologic and hydraulic analysis
SWMM (EPA) – for urban drainage and stormwater modeling
Navisworks / Revit / BIM 360 – for coordination and BIM workflows
Bluebeam Revu – for markup and document review
Microsoft Excel & Word – for calculations, reporting, and documentation
GIS (ArcGIS or QGIS) – for spatial analysis and mapping

Other duties:

Please note this job posting is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Civil Engineering from an accredited institution
Minimum 6 years of experience in civil infrastructure design, ideally within a consultancy and marine/port environment
Strong proficiency in AutoCAD Civil 3D and drainage design tools
Proven ability to manage design deliverables across multiple projects and meet deadlines
Chartered status or working toward chartership with ICE or equivalent body

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in port and terminal design, including pavement design, stormwater management, and utility coordination
Familiarity with BIM workflows, GIS, and marine infrastructure standards
Chartered status or working toward chartership with ICE or equivalent body
All new hires will be required to successfully complete and pass a pre-employment (post offer) background check in compliance with NIST 800-171

Working conditions:

Office-based role with occasional travel to client meetings or project sites
Extended hours may be required to meet project deadlines

Health & Safety Responsibilities:



All employees are expected to support a safe working environment by following health and safety laws, company policies, and procedures. This includes identifying and reporting risks, promoting safe practices, and contributing to continuous improvement in workplace safety.

Physical requirements:

Majority of work is office based. Some site visits may be required which may necessitate navigating unpaved or natural terrain while carrying equipment.


Moffatt & Nichol’s EEO Statement:

As a global business, Moffatt & Nichol relies on diversity of culture and thought to deliver on our goal of Creative People, Practical Solutions® serving our client needs, and ensures nondiscrimination in all activities in accordance with The Equality Act 2010. Applicants must have the legal right to work in the UK. Visa sponsorship may be considered for exceptional candidates. We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace. If you require any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know. We draw from the world’s best and brightest, regardless of geographic, cultural, or institutional background. This approach enables us to better understand and serve our clients, foster innovation, and create a rich, collaborative work environment. Moffatt & Nichol is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity / Federal Contractor desiring priority referrals of all protected veterans for job openings.

At APM Terminals, a global leader in port and terminal operations, we enable global trade and drive sustainable growth. As part of the A.P. Purpose:
Operational excellence is one of the four core strategic pillars of APM Terminals’ long-term strategy: “This is our platform for current and new terminals, where all value streams are standard, scalable, and replicable from acquisition, to construction, integration and all the way down to running a terminal”. Implementing, optimizing and scaling Operations Technology will play a pivotal role in achieving our strategic goals, ranging from initiatives like implementing complex brownfield automation at fast pace to
optimize manhours per move or efforts to optimize running-hours per move by deploying asset digitization in our more conventional terminals. To be successful, our terminals need to be supported by a focused, aligned and agile global team that adds value by Subject Matter Expertise, standardization and agile decision making.



As part of the Asset Digitization (AD) Solutions team within the Process Automation (Operational Technology) function, the SCADA Solutions Lead is responsible for leading, managing, and supporting the AD SCADA Solutions team, as well as overseeing all developments within its scope. This role owns and maintains the backend logic developed using SCADA scripting and coding tools, manages embedded databases within the SCADA environment, and ensures the standardization of AD visual and frontend solutions across terminals. The SCADA Solutions Lead also ensures that sourced signal mappings meet the expected quality standards in alignment with the latest agreed UDT specifications. Additional responsibilities include reporting project status, ensuring the timely delivery of roadmap milestones, and providing Level 3 support.

Coordinate and supervise the development of standardized SCADA products.

Co-design AD products in collaboration with AD Subject Matter Experts and aligned with AD strategy principles: SCADA empowerment, strong digital foundations, modularity, scalability, agility, and cost-effectiveness. Organize and oversee Level 3 (L3) product support when required.



Principal Accountabilities:

Promote Operations Technology and Automation as strategic enablers of safety and operational excellence.

Who We Are and Who We Serve

UMGC—one of 12 degree-granting institutions in the University System of Maryland (USM)—is a mission-driven institution with seven core values that guide us in all we do. At the top of the list is "Students First,” and we strive to do just that for our 90,000 students at home and abroad. From its start in 1947, UMGC has demonstrated its commitment to adult learners. We recognize that adult students need flexibility and options. UMGC is proud to be a global, 24-hour, institution of higher learning.

The typical UMGC student is an adult learner juggling a career, family, and other priorities. Roughly 80% work full time, half are parents, and half are minority students. They are continuing their education to better themselves, their families, and their professional opportunities. UMGC is also a leading higher education provider to the U.S. military, enrolling 55,000 active-duty service members, reservists, National Guard members, veterans, and family members annually. We are proud of our military heritage and are committed to this service.

The Adjunct Faculty Role at UMGC

UMGC is committed to helping students achieve success not only with us, but also in their professional fields. As a result, we actively seek faculty members who are scholar-practitioners: professionals who are actively and successfully engaged in their field who additionally wish to help the next generation of professionals grow in their knowledge and expertise through education. Your role as an adjunct faculty member will be to:

Actively engage students through frequent interaction that motivates them to succeed, and conveys a genuine energy and enthusiasm for their learning.

Guide students in active collaboration and the application of their learning in problem- and project-based learning demonstrations.

Provide rich and regular constructive feedback, utilizing rubrics effectively for the assessment of student work, and acknowledging student accomplishments.

Demonstrate relevant and current subject-matter expertise, and help students connect concepts across their academic program.

Provide feedback to your program chair on possible curricular improvements.

At APM Terminals, a global leader in port and terminal operations, we enable global trade and drive sustainable growth. As part of the A.P. Purpose:
Operational excellence is one of the four core strategic pillars of APM Terminals’ long-term strategy: “This is our platform for current and new terminals, where all value streams are standard, scalable, and replicable from acquisition, to construction, integration and all the way down to running a terminal”. Implementing, optimizing and scaling Operations Technology will play a pivotal role in achieving our strategic goals, ranging from initiatives like implementing complex brownfield automation at fast pace to optimize manhours per move or efforts to optimize running-hours per move by deploying asset digitization in our more conventional terminals. To be successful, our terminals need to be supported by a focused,
aligned and agile global team that adds value by Subject Matter Expertise, standardization and agile decision making. As part of the Asset Digitization (AD) Solutions team within the Process Automation (Operations Technology) function, the Assets & Industrial Connectivity Engineer plays a key role in enabling the physical connection of assets to our digital platform. The engineer assesses each asset type and determines the most effective connectivity method, based on business needs and infrastructure constraints. If a suitable solution does not exist, the engineer collaborates with AD and PA Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) to identify or develop one. This role is also responsible for creating comprehensive technical (hardware)
documentation, from installation guides to testing templates, to ensure deployments are repeatable and high quality. Additionally, the engineer supports terminal standardization efforts, performs signal mapping, contributes to a consistent data strategy, and assists to the team across a wide range of activities. Focus areas/main deliverables: Select and define the most effective connectivity approach for each asset. Develop and maintain comprehensive technical documentation and installation standards. Propose and support the standardization of solutions across terminals. Execute roadmaps, prepare documentation and provide technical support. Test and implement AD products in close collaboration with AD Subject
Matter Experts, ensuring alignment with core AD strategic principles: SCADA empowerment, strong digital foundations, modularity, scalability, agility, and cost-effectiveness.

Key stakeholders:

Terminals, OEMs/suppliers, OT Tech, OT Process Automation, OT Optimisation, OT Process Design & Management, Operational Excellence, GLASS/Automation Support, Project Execution, Labour Excellence, Decarbonisation, FLOW, Lean, Asset Maintenance, OPS, HSSE and Finance.



Principal Accountabilities:

Enable data flow from asset to servers.
Define and document Asset Digitization technical requirements to establish connections between terminal assets and the Asset Digitization application
Audit terminals to define technical needs, action plans and applicable technical requirements prior to project implementation.
Configuration of hardware devices to enable data flow.
Transfer knowledge and conduct training for terminal staff.
Define User Define Templates per equipment type (QCs, RTGs, TTs, RMGs, SCs, reefer containers, among others)
Ensure all relevant signals are classified according to User Defined Templates per equipment type, with proper documentation
Communicate and coordinate signal mapping tasks and activities with OEMs.
Once a terminal meets the prerequisites and connects the assets, it must ensure that the defined signals are available and accessible within the Ignition platform (commissioning and testing).
Install or retrofit hardware and sensors to enable new signals, in alignment with the User Defined Template.
Supervise and support the deployment of AD solutions across terminals in the portfolio, collaborating with internal stakeholders, external suppliers, and OEMs.
Present solutions and associated hardware to terminal stakeholders, deliver training, and oversee their independent execution of present/future deployments.
Provide different levels of support to AD products depending on the deployment status. (Before/After Go-Live).
Participate in Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT) and Site Acceptance Testing (SAT) and validate outcomes.
Support suppliers and OEMs in preparing relevant documentation for products and projects, including reviewing and approving materials.
Contribute to the design, development and implementation of the hardware architecture required for AD products.


Shared responsibilities:

Drive product performance and roadmap delivery in collaboration with the AD team.
Develop and execute QA/test cases across AD, Operational Technology (OT), Product Development & Manufacturing (PD&M), and Terminal environments.
Support and contribute to the creation of documentation for new products, including requirements and deployment materials, alongside AD and Process Automation (PA) Leads.
Ensure adherence to IT and OT standards in coordination with the Technology team.
Facilitate regulatory compliance and support audit processes, working with AD and PA Leads.
Lead change management efforts and promote product adoption across AD, OT PM&D, Terminal, and Operations functions.
Provide training to end users in collaboration with the AD team.


Critical Qualifications/Skills/Experience:
Minimum of 5 years of experience in products, projects or programs within technology and/or operations, preferably involving automation and/or the transport and logistics sector.
Hands-on experience in real-time operating or industrial environments (e.g. logistics, shipping, or port operations).
Strong understanding of port equipment, automated systems and controls, and industrial infrastructure architecture.
PLC: Intermediate to expert-level proficiency in programming and troubleshooting PLC systems across various brands, including Siemens, ABB, Yaskawa, Fuji, Omron, GE Fanuc and/or others.
Solid experience in industrial network protocols, device communication, IoT integration, field devices, edge computing, and relevant programming languages.
Intermediate to expert-level experience in managing and troubleshooting network infrastructure, servers, firewalls, infrastructure virtualization, databases, backup systems, and archiving policies.
Knowledge of cybersecurity practices in industrial and IoT environments.
Strong ability to work collaboratively in a team, solve problems, and adapt to new technologies.
Demonstrated strategic and critical thinking, including stakeholder and requirements management, business analysis, and product optimization.
Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset.
Extensive experience with SCADA systems, especially Ignition. Certification is preferred or expected within six months of onboarding.
Proficient in programming languages such as C/C++, Python, and Linux.
Experience with cloud IoT platforms or big data ecosystems is considered a plus.
Lean-minded: we are looking for someone who brings a strong mindset of continuous improvement – someone who is naturally curious, committed to solving problems at the root, and driven to make things better every day.


Skills:
Collaborative team player with a proven ability to motivate peers and direct reports toward shared success.
Excellent communication skills, both oral and written, in English.
Solid proficiency in standard professional software (e.g., MS Office, ERP/CMMS systems).
Persuasive and influential, with a talent for aligning colleagues and vendors around functional and project goals.
Highly driven, self-motivated, and results oriented.
Strong sense of discipline, committed to follow-through and comprehensive documentation.
Skilled in inspiring and developing teams to foster a performance-driven organization.
High commitment to quality and precision, with a meticulous working style.
Culturally sensitive, with hands-on experience in multicultural and international team environments.
Accountable for delivering milestones and maintaining project alignment.

Engineering degree or similar.
Ignition Certification desired.

University of Maryland Global Campus (UMGC) seeks adjunct faculty to teach on-site in Spain in the Sociology and Behavioral Sciences programs.

Required Education and Experience:

Master's degree in Sociology, Behavioral Sciences, or related field from an accredited institution of higher learning.

Professional experience in Sociology, Behavioral Sciences, or related field.

Experience teaching adult learners online and in higher education is strongly preferred.

Local applicants only. Adjunct faculty positions are not eligible for UMGC sponsored logistical support. Applicants must have and be able to maintain their own legal status in the country in which they apply to teach.

Resume/Curriculum Vitae

Cover letter highly preferred

Unofficial transcripts for all conferred degrees with conferral date

If selected, candidates with international degrees may be required to submit a translation/degree evaluation from a NACES approved vendor.

Who We Are and Who We Serve

UMGC—one of 12 degree-granting institutions in the University System of Maryland (USM)—is a mission-driven institution with seven core values that guide us in all we do. At the top of the list is "Students First,” and we strive to do just that for our 90,000 students at home and abroad. From its start in 1947, UMGC has demonstrated its commitment to adult learners. We recognize that adult students need flexibility and options. UMGC is proud to be a global, 24-hour, institution of higher learning.

The typical UMGC student is an adult learner juggling a career, family, and other priorities. Roughly 80% work full time, half are parents, and half are minority students. They are continuing their education to better themselves, their families, and their professional opportunities. UMGC is also a leading higher education provider to the U.S. military, enrolling 55,000 active-duty service members, reservists, National Guard members, veterans, and family members annually. We are proud of our military heritage and are committed to this service.

The Adjunct Faculty Role at UMGC

UMGC is committed to helping students achieve success not only with us, but also in their professional fields. As a result, we actively seek faculty members who are scholar-practitioners: professionals who are actively and successfully engaged in their field who additionally wish to help the next generation of professionals grow in their knowledge and expertise through education. Your role as an adjunct faculty member will be to:

Actively engage students through frequent interaction that motivates them to succeed, and conveys a genuine energy and enthusiasm for their learning.

Guide students in active collaboration and the application of their learning in problem- and project-based learning demonstrations.

Provide rich and regular constructive feedback, utilizing rubrics effectively for the assessment of student work, and acknowledging student accomplishments.

Demonstrate relevant and current subject-matter expertise, and help students connect concepts across their academic program.

Provide feedback to your program chair on possible curricular improvements.

https://europe.umgc.edu/about/careers-at-umgc-europe.html

Faculty Training at UMGC

We are committed to your professional success at UMGC. Each new faculty member is required to successfully complete our online two-week new faculty orientation, FacDev 122, as a condition of hire.

In the technical functional task area, SEA 21 requires contractor support in the areas of hull, mechanical, and electrical (HM&E), combat systems, aviation and data, cyber, and information systems. SEA 21 needs HM&E, combat systems, and aviation support for the program offices to plan modernization, analyze system performance across ships, and plan sustainment of systems. SEA 21 needs HM&E, combat systems, and aviation support at the waterfront to execute program modernization and work closely with fleet and the shore establishment to assess maintenance and sustainment of equipment at the individual ship level. SEA 21 needs data, cyber, and information systems support to design and assess the performance of systems and equipment during design and development, test events, trials, and normal operation. Technical efforts include analyzing reliability, maintainability, and availability data, developing and performing test and evaluation plans, and developing and implementing science and technology and SBIR efforts. Job Description


A chef in a luxury hotel has a range of key responsibilities and functions that go beyond just cooking. Their role involves ensuring that every aspect related to gastronomy is of the highest quality, maintaining a high level of service and creativity.

Responsibilities

Menu Design and Planning

Create exclusive menus: Develop innovative and sophisticated menus that reflect the identity of the hotel, considering guest preferences and culinary trends.

Adapt the menu to special needs: Create options for people with dietary restrictions (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, etc.) and ensure there are choices for all tastes.

Kitchen Team Management and Supervision

Lead the kitchen team: Supervise, motivate, and coordinate the kitchen staff (sous chefs, cooks, pastry chefs, etc.), fostering cooperation and a good work environment.

Train the staff: Ensure that the entire team is trained in cooking techniques, hygiene and food safety standards, and maintain a professional attitude.

Manage team performance: Evaluate the performance of staff and provide feedback to improve service quality.

Quality Control and Standards

Maintain the hotel’s culinary standards: Ensure that all dishes leaving the kitchen meet the hotel’s luxury standards in terms of flavor, presentation, and ingredient quality.

Review and adjust: Oversee the preparation and presentation of food, ensuring it meets customer expectations.

Inventory and Supplier Management

Control food and beverage inventories: Manage the stock of fresh ingredients, ensuring there is always an adequate supply of high-quality products without wastage.

Select quality suppliers: Negotiate with suppliers to obtain the best available ingredients, ensuring product quality, freshness, and exclusivity.

Compliance with Safety and Hygiene Regulations

Ensure food safety: Implement and oversee hygiene policies in the kitchen to comply with local and international food safety regulations.

Control allergens: Ensure food is properly labeled and minimize the risk of cross-contamination, protecting guests with food allergies.

Cost Optimization and Profitability

Manage the kitchen budget: Control ingredient costs, reduce waste, and ensure the team maintains profitability without compromising quality.

Maximize kitchen service profitability: Look for ways to improve kitchen efficiency, increasing profitability through proper resource management.

Customer Service and Culinary Experience

Personalize the culinary experience: In a luxury hotel, the chef may design custom menus for VIP clients, private dinners, or special events, providing an exclusive experience.

Interact with guests: Occasionally, the chef may be present during special dinners or events, offering a more direct experience with clients.

Special Event Management

Organize banquets and events: Supervise the preparation and execution of special events such as weddings, gala dinners, or conferences, ensuring everything meets the client’s expectations.

Exclusive catering services: Offer luxury catering services, adapting to the specific needs of each event, such as private dinners or exclusive menus.

Culinary Innovation

Develop new recipes: Create new techniques, flavors, and presentations to surprise guests and keep their interest in the culinary offerings.

Stay on top of culinary trends: Keep informed about the latest culinary trends and adapt them to meet the needs and expectations of luxury hotel guests.

Collaboration with Other Departments

Coordinate with other departments: Work closely with other hotel departments, such as customer service, sales, and events, to ensure that the culinary experiences align with the hotel’s image.

Advice on the drinks menu: Collaborate with the sommelier and bar team to create proper food and beverage pairings.

About the Role:
In the technical functional task area, SEA 21 requires contractor support in the areas of hull, mechanical, and electrical (HM&E), combat systems, and aviation and data, cyber, and information systems. SEA 21 needs HM&E, combat systems, and aviation support for the program offices to plan modernization, analyze system performance across ships, and plan sustainment of systems. SEA 21 needs HM&E, combat systems, and aviation support at the waterfront to execute program modernization and work closely with fleet and the shore establishment to assess maintenance and sustainment of equipment at the individual ship level. SEA 21 needs data, cyber, and information systems support to design and assess the performance of systems and equipment during design and development, test events, trials, and normal operation. Technical efforts include analyzing reliability, maintainability, and availability data, developing and performing test and evaluation plans, and developing and implementing science and technology and SBIR efforts. In support of this functional task area, the contractor must:

Analyze, manipulate, or process large sets of data using statistical software.

Evaluate performance of ships and systems during testing and dock and sea trials to determine conformance with design standards.

Descripcion_Completa:
Eduployment ofrece grandes oportunidades para profesionales de enfermería en los Países Bajos.

* DEBE ESTAR DISPUESTO/A A TRASLADARSE A LOS PAÍSES BAJOS PARA APROVECHAR ESTA OPORTUNIDAD *

El salario indicado es neto al mes.

EDUPLOYMENT

En Eduployment creemos fervientemente en la idea de que las barreras lingüísticas no deben frenar tu carrera profesional como enfermero/a. Por eso, desde 2002, nos dedicamos a enseñar neerlandés a profesionales de enfermería cualificados de la UE. Pero ofrecemos mucho más que clases de idiomas. También organizamos alojamiento a precios asequibles y proporcionamos formación completa, apoyo práctico y nos ocupamos de todas las tareas administrativas relacionadas con tu traslado. De este modo, podrás concentrarte en lo que mejor sabes hacer: ofrecer unos cuidados de enfermería excelentes.

¿Eres enfermero/a con cualificaciones de la UE? Siga leyendo y descubra lo que podemos ofrecerte.

LOS PAÍSES BAJOS

Los Países Bajos son un país lleno de canales y molinos de viento, famoso por su sistema educativo y su diseño. En los Países Bajos siempre estarás cerca de la naturaleza y de la vida urbana. Tanto si quieres hacer senderismo o montar en bicicleta por los bosques, explorar los cascos antiguos de las ciudades, tomar algo con los amigos, navegar o hacer surf en nuestros famosos ríos y lagos, o relajarte en las dunas y en las costas abiertas, los Países Bajos ofrecen mucho espacio para la exploración y una gran vida social. El país es conocido por su saludable equilibrio entre vida laboral y familiar y su sociedad bien organizada, caracterizada por altos niveles de confianza, bienestar y diversidad cultural.

PROFESIONALES DE ENFERMERÍA

Buscamos diplomados en enfermería dispuestos a desempeñar un papel activo en el cuidado no sólo de la salud física de los pacientes, sino también de su bienestar social, emocional y psicológico en el sector sanitario público neerlandés. Nuestras instituciones sanitarias asociadas te ofrecen un contrato de trabajo directo y a tiempo completo con excelentes paquetes salariales y de beneficios, fantásticas oportunidades para mejorar su CV y un plan de desarrollo personal a medida para impulsar tu carrera hacia nuevas cotas.

TE OFRECEMOS:

Formación en neerlandés (B1/B2).
Asistencia completa para el traslado: alojamiento (vivienda individual), servicio de recogida en el aeropuerto, apertura de cuenta bancaria, solicitud de seguro (de enfermedad), ayuda con el registro BIG (registro nacional neerlandés de asistencia sanitaria) e incluso compensación para el billete de avión (250 EUR como máximo).
Contratos de trabajo directos con la institución sanitaria (semana laboral de 36 horas).
Salario competitivo basado en el convenio colectivo Zorg & Welzijn.
Amplio paquete de prestaciones y compensación adicional por horas extraordinarias y trabajo irregular.
Numerosas oportunidades de especialización.
Un plan de desarrollo personal a medida que incluye formación adicional en idiomas y formación especializada durante el horario laboral, todo pagado por la institución sanitaria.
Acceso a una red nacional y a una plataforma de instituciones sanitarias para progresar en otras disciplinas.
Apoyo, asesoramiento y formación continuos.

QUÉ ESEPERAMOS DE TI? TU:

posees una licenciatura en enfermería reconocida por la UE.
dominas el inglés (nivel básico).
puedes comprometerte con nuestra formación lingüística a tiempo completo o parcial para alcanzar un nivel B1 de neerlandés.
tienes ganas de vivir una aventura que mejore tu CV y de invertir en ti mismo para adquirir experiencia laboral internacional.
tienes un interés genuino en trabajar con pacientes y muchas ganas de aprender.
tienes una actitud orientada al servicio y buenas dotes de comunicación.

Preferible CV en inglés.

Eduployment offers great opportunities for nursing professionals in the Netherlands!

* YOU MUST BE WILLING TO RELOCATE TO THE NETHERLANDS IN ORDER TO AVAIL OF THIS OPPORTUNITY *

The indicated salary is net per month.

EDUPLOYMENT

At Eduployment, we're passionate believers in the idea that language barriers shouldn't hold back your nursing career. That's why, since 2002, we've been dedicated to teaching Dutch to EU-qualified nursing professionals. But we offer so much more than language lessons. We also arrange affordable accommodation and provide comprehensive coaching, hands-on support and handle all the nitty-gritty administrative tasks regarding your relocation.

This way, you're free to concentrate on what you excel at - delivering excellent nursing care. Are you a nurse with EU qualifications? Keep on reading and see what we can offer you!

THE NETHERLANDS

The Netherlands is a country full of canals and windmills, renowned for its education system and design. In the Netherlands, you will always be close to nature and city life. Whether you want to hike or bike in forests, explore the old city centres, have a drink with friends, sail or surf on our famous rivers and lakes, or unwind in the dunes and on the open coastlines, the Netherlands offers plenty of space for exploration and a great social life. The country is known for its healthy work-life balance and well-organised society, characterised by high levels of trust, well-being, and cultural diversity.

NURSING GRADUATES

We are seeking nursing graduates ready to play an active role in nurturing not only the physical health of patients, but also their social, emotional, and psychological well-being within in the Dutch public healthcare sector. Our associated healthcare institutions offer you a direct, full-time employment contract with great salary & benefit packages, fantastic CV-boosting opportunities and a tailor-made Personal Development Plan to boost your career to new heights!

WE OFFER YOU:

An online Dutch language training (B1/B2) from the comfort of your home.
Complete relocation support: housing (individual housing) is arranged, airport pickup service, opening bank account, (health) insurance application, assistance with BIG-registration (Dutch national registry for healthcare workers) and even airfare compensation.
Direct employment contract with a healthcare institution (36-hour working week).
Competitive salary based on the collective labour agreement (NL: Zorg & Welzijn).
Comprehensive benefits package and additional compensation for overtime and working irregular hours.
Many opportunities for specialisation.
A tailor-made Personal Development Plan including additional language training and specialisation training during working hours, all paid for by the healthcare institution.
Access to a country-wide network and platform of healthcare institutions for advancement in other disciplines.
Continuous support, coaching and training.

WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

You have an EU-acknowledged bachelor’s degree in nursing.
You are proficient in English (basic level).
You can commit yourself to our full-time or part-time online language training to reach a B1 level in the Dutch language.
You have the desire for a CV-boosting adventure and want to invest in yourself by gaining international work experience.
You have a genuine interest in working with patients and you’re eager to learn.
You possess a service-oriented attitude and good communication skills.

CV in English is preferable.
